Examples

  • Gallery

ordinal

ordinal maps a categorical column to a discrete range indexed by category rank. It trains an indexed category table (value, idx, n) in SQL, then applies a keyed join to look up each row's rank and index into a range — a palette on a colour channel (fill/stroke), or a discrete position on a positional channel. The range is a parameter, not baked-in colour: ordinal is binding-polymorphic, so the output space is decided by the channel the instance serves (style⟨color⟩ → palette, relative⟨x|y⟩ → position). Single output, so it binds like linear — fill: ordinal("species"), no .pick().

const v = canvas(640, 460);
v.dot("penguins", {
  x: linear("bill_length_mm"),
  y: linear("flipper_length_mm"),
  fill: ordinal("species"),   // Adelie / Chinstrap / Gentoo → tableau10[0..2]
});
return v;

A scatter where the three species get the first three distinct hues of the default categorical palette (tableau10).

Options

ordinal(column, opts?). The column is the categorical field whose distinct values form the domain. On a colour channel the range is the resolved scheme palette; the default is tableau10.

Option Type Default Meaning
scheme scheme name "tableau10" named categorical palette (see below); resolved once to a fixed hue array
range array — explicit palette/positions — an author override that wins over the scheme
shared boolean — reuse the object across bindings to declare "same categories" (one unioned domain, one legend)
domain domain treatment "data" domain treatment (recorded intent)
axis AxisOptions | false — tick/label config when the scale is positional

The domain is categorical — the N-row indexed table (value, idx, n), JOINed on the value column (shape keyed), set-unionable, ordinal ticks, unordered by default (categories carry no positional meaning). Category rank indexes the range mod n, so a palette shorter than the domain cycles.

Categorical schemes

Distinct fixed-length hue arrays (from src/lib/dvl/components/schemes.ts), indexed by category rank:

Scheme Hues
tableau10 (default) #4e79a7 #f28e2b #e15759 #76b7b2 #59a14f #edc949 #af7aa1 #ff9da7 #9c755f #bab0ab
observable10 #4269d0 #efb118 #ff725c #6cc5b0 #3ca951 …
category10 #1f77b4 #ff7f0e #2ca02c #d62728 #9467bd …

The fallback when no scheme resolves is CATEGORICAL_PALETTE (tableau10-ish, in scales/_shared.ts).

Examples

Ordinal fill — the canonical categorical colour encoding:

const v = canvas(640, 460);
v.dot("penguins", {
  x: linear("bill_length_mm"),
  y: linear("body_mass_g"),
  fill: ordinal("species"),
});
return v;

Ordinal on the rect mark (same encoding, squares instead of dots):

const v = canvas(640, 460);
v.rect("penguins", {
  x: linear("bill_length_mm"),
  y: linear("body_mass_g"),
  w: 10, h: 10,
  fill: ordinal("species"),
});
return v;

Pick a different categorical scheme:

const v = canvas(640, 460);
v.dot("penguins", {
  x: linear("bill_length_mm"),
  y: linear("flipper_length_mm"),
  fill: ordinal("species", { scheme: "observable10" }),
});
return v;

Explicit palette via range — override the scheme with your own hues:

const v = canvas(640, 460);
v.dot("penguins", {
  x: linear("bill_length_mm"),
  y: linear("flipper_length_mm"),
  fill: ordinal("species", { range: ["#1b9e77", "#d95f02", "#7570b3"] }),
});
return v;

Ordinal driving stroke instead of fill:

const v = canvas(640, 460);
v.dot("penguins", {
  x: linear("bill_length_mm"),
  y: linear("body_mass_g"),
  stroke: ordinal("species"),
});
return v;

Ordinal for position — ordinal on a positional channel yields a discrete coordinate (categories placed at range slots). For evenly-spaced, band-centered positions prefer point; for a category → x-band-plus-width use the bandX layout:

// categorical fill + a positional band for x — the usual bar recipe.
const bySpecies = db.table("penguins").groupby(["species"], { mean_mass: avg("body_mass_g") });
const v = canvas(640, 460);
v.bar(bySpecies, {
  ...bandX("species", { paddingInner: 0.2 }).pick("x", "w"),
  ...barY("mean_mass").pick("y", "h"),
  fill: ordinal("species"),   // colour matches the x category
});
return v;

Categorical legend — ask the channel for its legend; the swatch set is lowered as an ordinary plot over the scale's domain:

const v = canvas(680, 460);
const p = v.dot("penguins", {
  x: linear("bill_length_mm"),
  y: linear("body_mass_g"),
  fill: ordinal("species"),
});
p.legend("fill", { placement: "right" });   // 3 swatches + labels in a right gutter
return v;

Legend as an in-plot overlay (consumes no layout space):

const v = canvas(640, 460);
const p = v.dot("penguins", {
  x: linear("bill_length_mm"),
  y: linear("flipper_length_mm"),
  fill: ordinal("species"),
});
p.legend("fill", { placement: "inside" });
return v;

See also

  • point — categories → evenly-spaced band-centered positions.
  • color — colour as an output space; continuous ramps vs. categorical palettes.
  • bandX — category → positional band with width.
